steps.explanation: Identify the growth model. A 3.5% decrease each year means each year's count is multiplied by \(1 - 0.035 = 0.965\). This is exponential decay with multiplier 0.965.
Count the years from 2005 to 2015: \(2015 - 2005 = 10\) years. The exponent on the multiplier is 10.
Write the formula. Starting from 6.22 pheasants per mile in 2005, after 10 years the count is \(6.22 \cdot (0.965)^{10}\).

Evaluate each choice.
  • Choice A: \(0.965(6.22)^{10}\) raises the initial value to the 10th power, not the multiplier. This is not exponential decay — eliminate A.
  • Choice B: \(1.035(6.22)^{10}\) uses the growth factor and again raises the wrong base. Eliminate B.
  • Choice C: \(6.22(0.035)^{10}\) uses 0.035 (the rate alone) as the base instead of \(1 - 0.035 = 0.965\). Eliminate C.
  • Choice D: \(6.22(0.965)^{10}\) is exactly the exponential decay formula with the correct starting value, correct multiplier, and correct exponent. That matches choice D — D is correct.
